title: tinus odontophylla
intro: Tinus odontophylla, also known as Toothache Tree, is a plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, yellowish flowers and red or purple fruit. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in treating toothaches and other oral health issues. The health benefits of Tinus odontophylla include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be made by drying the leaves and bark, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of oral health issues.
